## Release — backlog-campaign v0.12.0

### ✨ What's new in v0.12.0

Minor release: **campaign routing is now visible, and code reuse is enforced before code is
written** (ADR-008). The router already computed a rich per-issue `route{}` (which issues need
research, design, or a full plan) — but that intelligence was invisible in the dashboard, and
reuse (V-INT) was only ever caught *after* a duplicate was written. This release surfaces the
routing and shifts reuse enforcement left. Decision record:
`documentation/decisions/ADR-008-routing-visibility-reuse-gate.md`.

#### Dashboard visibility

- **Routing section** — the status dashboard now shows, per active issue, its **planned
  conditional chain** `Handle → [research?] → [investigate?] → [design-gate?] → Plan(tier) →
  Implement → Review([security?])` with the current phase marked and per-flag confidence. You can
  finally see at a glance which issues need more research, design, or planning.
- **Waves section** — execution waves are now rendered from a dependency topological sort
  (behaviorally identical to the scheduler's own Step 4), so you can monitor that every issue is
  progressing through its steps. Dependency cycles surface in an explicit *Unresolved* line rather
  than being silently dropped.
- Honest by design: the dashboard shows the **planned chain + current position**, not a
  reconstructed history (the route object carries no transition log).

#### Proactive reuse enforcement

- **Reuse Check gate (implementer)** — before writing any code, the implementer now greps the
  issue's touch-paths for an existing utility/convention and records a one-line `Reuse Check:`
  entry in the PR body (mirroring the existing Bugfix-Gate / Drift-Check artifact pattern).
- **Reviewer verification + live-grep fallback** — the reviewer verifies that Reuse Check entry
  (BLOCK if absent) and, when a plan carries no `Codebase Conventions`, runs a live grep so the
  `V-INT-01/03/04` convention audit stops silently no-oping on Quick-track issues.

#### Rollout

- Documented a one-time **router re-triage backfill** (open, non-in-flight issues only) so the new
  Routing section populates on the standing queue without racing active workers.

The planner and the `queue.json` schema are intentionally untouched — the change stays additive.

---

**Full feature overview:** see [v0.1.0 release notes](https://github.com/CorentinLumineau/blackhole/releases/tag/v0.1.0) · [README](https://github.com/CorentinLumineau/blackhole#readme)
